title: GitHub Markdown Publish Bridge Blueprint
lead_source: Public GitHub issue asks for a way to push Markdown files from an editor to a GitHub repo and says the requester can pay for the feature: https://github.com/marktext/marktext/issues/3831. No external comment was posted.
acceptance_statement: I accept the GitHub Markdown Publish Bridge Blueprint fixed-scope terms at $100. I understand work starts only after seller-owned external payment proof exists; I will provide only public or buyer-authorized non-sensitive product requirements; the deliverable is limited to a public-safe implementation blueprint, GitHub API/OAuth scope checklist, UX flow, branch/commit strategy, error-handling plan, and test checklist; and production OAuth app setup, private repo access, credentials, token handling, deployed code, marketplace approval, ongoing support, or extra revisions are not included unless separately agreed before payment.
response_draft_not_posted: I saw this request and can offer a narrow fixed-scope GitHub Markdown Publish Bridge Blueprint for $100. I would not need GitHub tokens, OAuth secrets, or private repo access in the public issue. The paid deliverable would be a practical implementation plan covering GitHub API choices, OAuth scopes, branch/commit flow, UI states, error cases, and test checklist, with production credential setup left to the project owner.
posting_boundary: The draft response is prepared but not posted. External public comments or direct outreach require explicit send approval.
money_rule: Count $0 until real buyer acceptance, seller-owned external payment proof, delivery proof, and posted/released/payable/cleared funds exist.
